payload.summary: The weekly review pulse highlights the need for improvement in User Experience and Interface (30%), Transaction and Trading Issues (40%), and Security, Support, and Fees (30%). Users have reported issues with the app's UI, difficulty with transactions, and poor customer support. Some users have also experienced losses due to technical issues and hidden charges.
quotes: The app and UI is awesome.
Extremely frustrating experience. Almost every time I added funds, it took 7–10 days for the amount to reflect in my US stock account.
DO NOT USE! Entire US Portfolio WIPED OUT to ZERO!
action_items: Improve the app's UI and user experience to make it more intuitive and user-friendly
Address transaction and trading issues, including delayed fund reflections and hidden charges
Enhance customer support and security measures to prevent losses and data breaches
action_ideas: Improve the app's UI and user experience to make it more intuitive and user-friendly
Address transaction and trading issues, including delayed fund reflections and hidden charges
Enhance customer support and security measures to prevent losses and data breaches

explanation_bullets: A mutual fund exit load is a fee charged to investors when they redeem their units from a mutual fund scheme.
The exit load is typically a percentage of the redemption amount and is deducted from the investor's proceeds.
The purpose of an exit load is to discourage investors from withdrawing their money too quickly, thereby helping the fund manager to maintain a stable portfolio.
The exit load percentage and the period for which it is applicable vary across different mutual fund schemes.
The exit load is usually highest for redemptions made within a short period, such as a few days or weeks, after investing.
The exit load structure is clearly disclosed in the mutual fund scheme's offer document and website.
A brokerage fee is a charge levied by brokers or financial institutions for facilitating transactions
It is typically a percentage of the transaction value or a flat fee
Brokerage fees can vary depending on the type of transaction, such as buying or selling securities
These fees are usually deducted from the transaction amount
Brokerage fees can be charged for various investment products, including stocks, mutual funds, and exchange-traded funds (ETFs)
The fee structure and rates are typically disclosed by the broker or financial institution

Extremely frustrating experience. Almost every time I added funds, it took 7–10 days for the amount to reflect in my US stock account. In the latest case, the amount was debited on 4th, SWIFT generated on 6th, yet funds are still not credited. Funds added on Monday remain pending even by Friday, causing loss of an entire trading week. Customer support is equally disappointing, with only bot-generated responses and no clarity on the root cause. Considering alternatives like Vested.

INDmoney offers useful features, but constant UI/UX changes make it frustrating to use. Frequent updates shift menus and navigation, forcing users to relearn the app again and again. Instead of improving usability, many changes create confusion and slow down simple tasks. A finance app should feel stable and predictable. Please focus on consistency and meaningful improvements rather than frequent redesigns.

The USD conversion via ICICI Bank is misleading. The exchange rate shown in the INDMoney app is not the rate actually applied—there’s a difference of about ₹0.30 per dollar, with the bank charging a higher rate. When I contacted ICICI Bank, they stated that since this is a third-party transaction, they cannot address the discrepancy. On the other hand, INDMoney’s support team was unable to provide a clear explanation for the difference in exchange rates.
